business.headline: Confused by the new tax law? Here are 6 sections that matter most to youThe Income Tax Act 2025 has introduced several updates, leaving many taxpayers unsure about what to track. But once you focus on the most relevant sections, the picture becomes much clearer.

Govt doubles states' quota of 5 kg LPG cylinders meant for migrant workersIn an order issued on April 6, the government said the daily quantity of 5 kg free trade LPG (FTL) cylinders available for distribution to migrant labourers will be doubled, based on the average daily number of cylinders supplied on March 2 and 3, 2026.

Ongoing tensions in the Middle East, including the US-Iran conflict, have already tightened the availability of oil and gas globally. This has put pressure on imports of crude oil, natural gas and LPG into India.

entertainment.headline: Ameesha Patel slams Zakir Khan's Dhurandhar 2 remark: SRK, Sunny created Gadar Ameesha Patel reacted to Zakir Khan's recent remarks about Hindi film industry being jealous of Dhurandhar. Badshah pledges women's empowerment song, education for 50 underprivileged girls Rapper Badshah pledged a women's empowerment track within four months amid legal scrutiny over his controversial song, Tateeree. He also promised to sponsor education for 50 underprivileged girls.

general news.headline: A whole civilisation will die tonight: Trump warns Iran as deadline loomsTrump still held his cards close to his chest, saying the attack might be called off at the last moment depending on the situation, and described tonight as "the most important moment in the long and complex history of the world."

Iran says no talks with US after Trump's big warning hours before deadline endsIran has cut off all direct and indirect communication channels with the US, hours after US President Donald Trump warned that "a whole civilisation will die" if Tehran failed to agree to a ceasefire by his deadline of 8 pm ET (5:30 am IST Wednesday).

Live: Netanyahu says IDF crushing Iran with increasing intensity ahead of deadlineUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: With no signs of the Middle East war abating, strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, a critical oil export hub handling most of the country's crude, have heightened fears over global energy supply and deepening regional tensions. Israel's military said it carried out a wide wave of attacks across Iran, including a petrochemical site in Shiraz, while blasts in Tehran reportedly killed at least 15 people. An Israeli strike also hit the Yahya Abad railway bridge in Kashan, killing two, after warnings urged civilians to avoid train travel. Meanwhile, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed retaliatory strikes targeting US-linked companies in Saudi Arabia, as cross-border hostilities intensified. Iranian projectiles reportedly struck areas in central Israel, causing damage. The King Fahd Causeway between Saudi Arabia and Bahrain has been shut indefinitely amid security concerns. Iran's ambassador to Pakistan described diplomatic efforts as reaching a "critical" stage. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Stay put for next 48 hours, avoid military sites: India to its citizens in IranThe advisory emphasises staying indoors and maintaining close coordination with the embassy, particularly for any highway travel, which should only be undertaken with official guidance.

BJP leader Himanta Biswa Sarma earlier used the passport issue to attack Congress's Gaurav Gogoi. The Congress's counter-strike has seen Assam police ending up at Pawan Khera's doorsteps. This is the larger issue behind the passport battle.

US hits 50 military targets on Iran's Kharg Island; Trump deadline hours awayThe US hit 50 military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's strategic oil hub, just hours before a deadline set by US President Donald Trump for Iran to strike a ceasefire deal ends at 8 pm ET on Tuesday (5:30 am IST on Wednesday).

Women can't be untouchable for 3 days a month: Supreme Court on Sabarimala caseJustice BV Nagarathna said Article 17 cannot be applied selectively, rejecting the idea of "temporary untouchability" for women. She was responding to SG Tushar Mehta, who objected to observations in the 2018 Sabarimala verdict equating the exclusion of menstruating women with treating them as "untouchables."

What US wants to achieve by invading Kharg islandWhy are the US and Israel targeting Kharg island? By seizing or crippling Kharg, the US aims to starve Iran of oil revenue, force concessions on its nuclear programme and regional influence. But the gamble risks spiralling oil prices and a wider regional war.

Trump admin's New York hotel pact with Pakistan raises stinkA US-Pakistan deal to redevelop New York's iconic Roosevelt Hotel has raised eyebrows and been called "unimaginable" by a real estate industry expert. While Pakistan said it aims to maximise the value of the property through the deal, the lack of key details on costs, control, and profit-sharing has got many alarmed.
